# Gcore Edge Cloud

- [Cloud](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud.md)
- [About Gcore cloud](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/about-gcore-cloud.md): Access Gcore Edge Cloud IaaS and PaaS services with on-demand resource delivery, pay-as-you-go pricing, and TIER III/IV data centers in Manassas, Amsterdam, ...
- [Bandwidth and traffic](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bandwidth-and-traffic.md): Compare bandwidth limits across Basic VMs (200 Mbps), Dedicated Instances (1 Gbps), and Bare Metal servers (10+ Gbps); all VMs include unlimited free ingress...
- [Billing](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/billing.md): Track Edge Cloud PAYG billing per-minute charges, balance thresholds (€4/$4), payment methods, service suspension policies, and resource-specific pricing for...
- [Tags](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/tags.md): Assign key-value tag pairs to Cloud resources via portal, API, Terraform, or SDK; filter resources by tags; enforce tag formatting rules (3-255 characters, n...

## Getting started
- [Request a quota increase](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/request-a-quota-increase.md): Request quota increases for compute, storage, network, and other Gcore Cloud resources via Quotas Viewer in Customer Portal by region and category.

## Projects
- [Create and manage projects](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/projects/create-a-project.md): Create, view, and manage isolated Edge Cloud projects with Virtual Machines, volumes, networks, and user permission controls via Customer Portal.

## Users
- [User roles and rights](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/projects/users/user-roles-and-rights.md): Define role-based access control with Client Administrator, Project Administrator, Project User, and Project Internal Network Only User roles for account and...
- [Add a new user to your project](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/projects/users/add-a-new-user-to-your-project.md): Invite users to Gcore projects via email with role assignment in Customer Portal Access settings.
- [Manage user access to your project](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/projects/users/manage-user-access-to-your-project.md): Edit user roles (User, Observer, Administrator), revoke project access via Delete role, and restore access by reassigning roles in project settings.
- [View user actions](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/projects/users/view-user-actions.md): View user action logs in Gcore Edge Cloud with filters for resource ID, project, action type, resource group, and time range.
- [Cost reports](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/view-statistics-on-expenses.md): View Cloud resource usage costs filtered by project, region, resource type, and consumption period using Pay-As-You-Go pricing model via Cost Report page or ...

## Resource reservation
- [About resource reservation](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/resource-reservation/about-resource-reservation.md): Reserve Cloud servers for 1 or 3 years at discounted rates via Gcore Customer Portal Reservations with region, server type, Windows License, and quantity con...

## Virtual Machines
- [Types of Virtual Machines](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/types-of-virtual-machines.md): Compare Gcore VM types: Standard, GPU, CPU, Memory, High-frequency, Basic, TPM, and Network-optimised instances with network bandwidth specifications.
- [Create a Virtual Machine](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/create-an-instance.md): Provision Linux or Windows Virtual Machines with region selection, x86-64/ARM architecture, OS image configuration, and CPU flavor selection in Gcore Cloud.
- [Customize the initial setup for your Virtual Machine](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/customize-initial-setup-for-your-instance.md): Configure VM initialization with User Data field using cloud-init YAML syntax via portal, Gcore API, or Terraform to automate password setup, user creation, ...
- [Check the operational status of your Virtual Machine](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/check-the-operational-status-of-your-instance.md): Monitor Virtual Machine operational statuses (Building, Power on, Power off, Error, Deleted) in Virtual Instances page Status column.
- [Enable root user on a Linux VM](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/enable-root-user-on-a-linux-vm.md): Enable root user on Linux VMs using sudo, sudo su, sudoers configuration, or interactive/non-interactive superuser shells via SSH or Control Panel.

## Connect
- [Connect to a Virtual Machine via the Gcore customer portal](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/connect/connect-to-your-instance-via-control-panel.md): Access Virtual Machines via VNC console in Gcore Customer Portal using password authentication for Windows and Linux instances.

## Connect via SSH
- [Connect to a Virtual Machine via SSH](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/connect/connect-via-ssh.md): Establish SSH connections to Gcore Linux Virtual Machines using SSH keys or password authentication with firewall configuration.
- [Configure and manage SSH keys](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/connect/connect-to-your-instance-via-ssh.md): Generate and manage SSH key pairs in Gcore Customer Portal; add public keys to VMs; store private keys locally for authentication.
- [Convert an SSH key to PPK format](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/ssh-keys/convert-an-ssh-key-from-pem-to-ppk.md): Convert SSH keys to PPK format using PuTTYgen for PuTTY connections to Gcore Virtual Machines.
- [Establish SSH connection to a Virtual Machine](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/connect/connect-via-ssh/establish-ssh-connection-to-an-instance.md): Connect to Virtual Machine instances using SSH keys or password authentication via terminal, command prompt, WSL, or PuTTY with floating IP and firewall conf...

## Placement groups
- [About placement groups](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/placement-groups/about-placement-groups.md): Configure Virtual Machine placement policies using affinity, anti-affinity, or soft anti-affinity groups to control physical server distribution and fault to...
- [Configure a placement group](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/placement-groups/configure-a-placement-group.md): Create and manage placement groups with affinity, anti-affinity, or soft anti-affinity policies to control Virtual Machine hardware placement and fault toler...

## Volumes
- [Volumes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/volumes/about-volumes.md): Create and manage cloud block storage volumes for Virtual Machines with boot/regular volume types, High IOPS SSD, Standard, and SSD Low-Latency options using...
- [Create and configure a volume](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/volumes/create-and-configure-volumes.md): Create and configure volumes in Gcore Customer Portal: specify size, volume type, attach to Virtual Machine, set IOPS/MBps limits, and manage metadata tags.
- [Monitor load on a specific node](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/monitor-load-and-performance-of-your-instance.md): Monitor VM performance metrics including CPU utilization, RAM usage, network BPS/PPS ingress/egress, and disk IOPS read/write operations via the Monitoring tab.

## Snapshots
- [Take a snapshot of your file system](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/snapshots/take-a-snapshot-of-your-file-system.md): Create volume snapshots from system or regular volumes via Snapshots, Volumes, or Instances pages for backup and VM deployment.
- [Set up automatic snapshot creation](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/snapshots/set-up-automatic-snapshot-creation.md): Configure Snapshot Schedules to automate volume snapshot creation with custom intervals, retention policies, and name templates using dynamic variables like ...

## Troubleshooting
- [Troubleshoot issues with an SSH connection](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/virtual-instances/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-issues-with-ssh-connection.md): Diagnose SSH connection failures including key authentication issues, root login restrictions, default usernames by OS, and VNC console password recovery for...

## Bare Metal servers
- [About Bare Metal](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bare-metal-servers/about-bare-metal-servers.md): Rent dedicated physical servers across 25 locations with exclusive CPU/memory access, configurable OS images, LACP-aggregated networking, and management via ...
- [Types of Bare Metal servers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bare-metal-servers/types-of-bare-metal-servers.md): Compare Bare Metal server types: High-frequency (Intel Xeon E-2388G, Ryzen 5950x), Infrastructure (dual Silver 4214R/4314), and Basic (Xeon E-2236) with CPU,...
- [Create a Bare Metal server](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bare-metal-servers/create-a-bare-metal-server.md): Deploy dedicated physical hardware Bare Metal servers via Cloud interface with region selection, image templates, and quota management for single-tenant high...
- [Connect to your Bare Metal via SSH](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bare-metal-servers/connect-to-your-bare-metal-server-via-ssh.md): Connect to Bare Metal servers via SSH using RSA key pairs or password authentication with firewall rules configuration.

## Troubleshooting
- [Troubleshoot issues with an SSH connection](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bare-metal-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-issues-with-ssh-connection.md): Diagnose SSH connection failures on Bare Metal servers including root login denial, missing credentials, lost keys, and SSH key authentication setup.

## Advanced DDoS protection
- [About advanced DDoS protection for Bare Metal](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bare-metal-servers/advanced-ddos-protection/about-advanced-ddos-protection-for-bare-metal-servers.md): Enable Advanced DDoS protection with threat mitigation system (TMS) for Bare Metal to filter L3/L4/L7 attacks, configure ACL, and maintain server uptime with...
- [Activate advanced DDoS protection for Bare Metal](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/bare-metal-servers/advanced-ddos-protection/activate-advanced-ddos-protection-for-bare-metal-servers.md): Enable Advanced DDoS protection on Bare Metal server network interfaces with public IPs by selecting protection profiles during server creation or via Networ...

## Images
- [About images](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/images/about-images.md): Create Virtual Machine boot volumes from disk images containing OS and pre-installed software.
- [Upload an image to the storage](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/images/upload-an-image-to-the-storage.md): Upload images to Gcore cloud storage via URL with support for raw, vhd, vhdx, vdi, ploop, qcow2, and AMI formats; configure VirtIO drivers, cloud-init, archi...

## Networking
- [Create and manage a network](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/create-and-manage-a-network.md): Create and attach private, public, or dedicated networks to VMs, Bare Metal, Load Balancers, and Kubernetes clusters using VxLAN or VLAN protocols.
- [Create and manage a subnetwork](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/create-and-manage-a-subnetwork.md): Create and configure subnetwork IP ranges, DHCP, DNS, host routes, and non-routable settings within Gcore cloud networks using CIDR notation (10.0.0.0/8, 172...
- [Create and manage a router](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/create-and-manage-a-router.md): Create and configure Cloud Router instances with static routes, SNAT, and subnet associations for dynamic route exchange between networks.

## IP address
- [Create and configure a floating IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/create-and-configure-a-floating-ip-address.md): Assign static public IP addresses to VMs, Bare Metal servers, and Load Balancers using floating IPs with subnet routing configuration and traffic forwarding.
- [Create and configure a reserved IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/create-and-configure-a-reserved-ip-address.md): Allocate dedicated public, private, or dedicated public IP addresses to Virtual Machines, Bare Metal servers, or Load Balancers with optional custom IPv4/IPv...
- [Create and configure a virtual IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/create-and-configure-a-virtual-ip-vip-address.md): Reserve and assign virtual IP addresses (VIP) to VMs using Keepalived and VRRP for fault tolerance or as secondary network interface addresses.
- [Difference between virtual, floating, and reserved IP addresses](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/difference-between-virtual-floating-and-reserved-ip-addresses.md): Compare Floating IP, Reserved IP, and Virtual IP address types in Gcore Cloud: DNAT mapping, pre-allocation, shared failover, assignment scope, billing, and ...
- [Configure an allowed address pair](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/configure-an-allowed-address-pair.md): Assign additional IP addresses to VM network interfaces using the allowed address pairs API endpoint with MAC-IP binding configuration.

## Load balancers
- [Create and configure a load balancer](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/create-and-configure-a-load-balancer.md): Configure Load Balancer region, computing resources (vCPU/GiB), network type (Public/Private/Dedicated), IP family (IPv4/IPv6/Dual), reserved IP assignment, ...
- [Add TLS certificates to a load balancer](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/add-certificates-to-load-balancer.md): Configure multiple TLS certificates on Load Balancer listeners using SNI to serve different domains on a single IP address via Terminated HTTPS or Prometheus...
- [Configure mutual TLS authentication](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/configure-mtls-authentication.md): Configure mutual TLS authentication between Gcore Load Balancers and backend servers using PKCS12/PEM certificates, CA verification, and mTLS pool parameters.
- [Manage load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/manage-load-balancers.md): View Load Balancer properties, statuses, listeners, IP addresses, regions, and tags in Customer Portal; monitor operating and provisioning status of Load Bal...
- [Logging for load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/logging-for-load-balancers.md): Configure Logging for Load Balancers with namespace and topic creation, including guaranteed throughput metrics across region pairs.
- [Use annotations for load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/use-annotations-for-load-balancers.md): Configure Load Balancer behavior in Gcore Managed Kubernetes using annotations for network type, flavor, IP assignment, listener timeouts, and pool node sele...
- [Monitoring for load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/monitoring-for-load-balancers.md): Configure Prometheus listeners on load balancers to expose metrics endpoints for scraping with Prometheus and Grafana visualization.
- [Add and configure a firewall](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/add-and-configure-a-firewall.md): Create firewall rules with inbound/outbound traffic policies, template rules (SSH/HTTP/HTTPS/MySQL/DNS/PostgreSQL/RDP), custom protocols/ports, CIDR sources,...
- [Bring Your Own IP (BYOIP)](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/byoip.md): Configure custom public IP address ranges with Gcore using BYOIP, requiring LOA, IRR Route Object, RPKI ROA, and CIDR prefix for IP ownership preservation an...
- [Cloud egress IP addresses](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/cloud-egress-ip-addresses.md): Retrieve and allowlist Gcore Cloud egress IP ranges (IPv4/IPv6 CIDR) via public API for webhooks, AMQP, asset delivery, and firewall rules.

## File shares
- [About file shares](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/file-shares/about-file-shares.md): Create and manage NFS file shares across VMs, Bare Metal, and Kubernetes pods with configurable access rules and snapshot recovery.
- [Configuring file shares](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/file-shares/configure-file-shares.md): Create NFS-based file shares (Standard or VAST) and mount them to Linux VMs, bare metal servers, or GPU clusters with network and access configuration.
- [Migrate data from S3 and EFS to Gcore](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/file-shares/how-to-migrate-data-from-s3.md): Migrate data from AWS S3 and EFS to Gcore Object Storage or File Shares using rclone with S3-compatible APIs and NFS mounts.

## Managed Kubernetes
- [About GCore Managed Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/about-gcore-kubernetes.md): Deploy Kubernetes clusters on VMs or Bare Metal with managed control planes, autoscaling, self-healing, PersistentVolumeClaims storage, and Nginx ingress net...
- [Bare Metal Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/bare-metal-kubernetes.md): Deploy Kubernetes worker nodes on Bare Metal servers with Managed Kubernetes for direct hardware access, eliminating virtualization overhead and improving pe...

## Clusters
- [Create a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/create-a-kubernetes-cluster.md): Configure Kubernetes cluster region, version, node pools with VM/Bare Metal flavors, disk types (High IOPS SSD, Standard, SSD Low Latency), autoscaling min/m...
- [Upgrade a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/upgrade.md): Upgrade Kubernetes cluster version using rolling updates with API compatibility checks and downgrade support for one patch version.

## Connect
- [Connect to your nodes via SSH](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/connect/connect-to-your-nodes-via-ssh-using-a-floating-ip-address.md): Assign floating IP addresses to Kubernetes nodes and connect via SSH using floating IP and private key authentication.
- [Install kubectl and connect to a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/connect/install-kubectl-and-connect-to-a-kubernetes-cluster.md): Install kubectl binary on Windows, configure PATH environment variable, and set up kubeconfig file in .kube directory for cluster access.
- [Secure Kubernetes cluster with OpenID Connect](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/secure-cluster-with-oidc.md): Configure OpenID Connect authentication for Kubernetes clusters with issuer URL, client ID, groups claim, signing algorithms, and username claim settings.

## Autoscaling
- [About autoscaling](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/about-autoscaling.md): Configure Kubernetes node pool autoscaling with minimum/maximum limits to automatically scale nodes based on pod resource requests and idle time thresholds.
- [Change autoscaling limits](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/change-autoscaling-limits.md): Modify minimum and maximum node counts for Kubernetes cluster autoscaling pools via Cloud console Pools section.
- [Create and configure a pool](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/create-a-new-pool-in-cluster.md): Create Kubernetes cluster pools with node configuration, autoscaling limits, VM/Bare Metal selection, placement policies, labels, taints, and autohealing set...
- [Advanced cluster autoscaler settings](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/advanced-cluster-autoscaler-settings.md): Configure Kubernetes cluster autoscaler parameters: scan interval, expander strategy (random, most-pods, least-waste, price, priority, gRPC), node provision ...
- [Configure GPU autoscaling for Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/configure-gpu-autoscaling-for-kubernetes.md): Install and configure KEDA for GPU-based autoscaling in Gcore Managed Kubernetes clusters with Bare Metal worker pools.
- [Add users with limited rights to a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/add-users-with-limited-rights-to-a-kubernetes-cluster.md): Create Kubernetes service accounts and namespaces with kubectl to grant users restricted API access via kubeconfig files scoped to specific namespaces.
- [Monitor load on a specific node](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/monitor-load-on-a-specific-node.md): Monitor CPU Utilization, RAM Utilization, Network Traffic, and Network Packets metrics for Kubernetes nodes via Monitoring tab in Cloud console.

## Networking
- [About networking in Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/networking/about-networking-in-kubernetes.md): Configure Kubernetes Services, Ingress objects, and Ingress Controllers to route internal pod traffic and external cluster traffic through single IP abstract...
- [Install nginx Ingress controller](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/networking/install-and-set-up-the-nginx-ingress-controller.md): Deploy nginx Ingress Controller on Managed Kubernetes using Helm, automatically provisioning a Load Balancer for traffic routing to services.
- [Use Nginx Ingress Controller](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/networking/use-the-nginx-ingress-controller.md): Deploy Nginx Ingress Controller using kubectl to create Ingress objects with ingressClassResource, Service routing, and port configuration.

## Storage
- [Create a PVC and bind to a pod](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/storage/create-a-pvc-and-bind-it-to-a-pod.md): Create PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) with StorageClass for standard, ssd_hiiops, or ssd_lowlatency disk types and bind to Kubernetes pods.
- [Configure NFS CSI driver for Managed Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/storage/configure-nfs-csi-driver-for-kubernetes.md): Configure NFS CSI driver to provision persistent volumes in Managed Kubernetes clusters using Gcore File Shares with dynamic attachment and management.
- [Troubleshooting a node in a "Not Ready" state](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/troubleshooting-not-ready-state.md): Diagnose and resolve Kubernetes nodes in "Not Ready" state by querying node conditions, checking Kubelet logs for certificate/authentication/network errors, ...

## Managed PostgreSQL
- [About managed PostgreSQL](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/managed-database-postgresql.md): Deploy and manage PostgreSQL 13/14/15 relational database instances with configurable flavor, storage, replication mode, and IP access control lists.
- [Manage a PostgreSQL server](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/managed-database-postgresql/manage-postgresql-servers.md): Create PostgreSQL servers with region, version, compute flavor, storage volume, and optional high-availability replication modes (asynchronous/synchronous).
- [Backup and restore PostgreSQL database for Managed PostgreSQL using external utilities](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/managed-database-postgresql/backup-and-restore-postgresql-database-for-gcore-managed-database-for-postgresql-using-external-utilities.md): Backup and restore Gcore Managed PostgreSQL databases using pg_dump and pg_restore tools with SQL, TAR, custom, or directory formats.

## Secrets manager
- [Configure secrets for HTTPS load balancer](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/secrets-manager/upload-a-pkcs12-file.md): Upload SSL/TLS certificates and private keys to Secrets Manager via PKCS12 file or manual entry for HTTPS load balancer listeners.

## Function as a service
- [About Function as a Service](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/faas/about-function-as-a-service.md): Deploy serverless functions via Gcore FaaS with automatic Kubernetes pod management, zero-scaler for idle resources, and HTTP endpoints for invocation.
- [Authenticate to functions with API keys](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/faas/authenticate-to-functions-with-api-keys.md): Create, edit, and delete API keys for Gcore Functions endpoints to enforce authentication and control function access permissions.
- [Code examples for supported FaaS runtimes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/faas/code-examples-for-supported-faas-runtimes.md): Implement FaaS functions in .NET, Go, Java, Node.js, and Python with code examples for basic functions, dependency management, and request/response handling.

## Container as a service
- [About container as a service](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/caas.md): Deploy and manage containerized applications without Kubernetes or VM infrastructure using Gcore CaaS via portal or REST API.
- [Create a container](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/caas/create-a-container.md): Deploy containerized applications via Container as a Service with image URL, registry credentials, port configuration, CPU/memory allocation, and autoscaling...
- [Build and deploy containers to CaaS via GitHub actions](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/caas/build-and-deploy-containers-to-caas-via-github-actions.md): Deploy containers to Gcore CaaS using GitHub Actions workflow with deploy-container action, configuring API token, project ID, region, image, port, environme...
- [Manage containers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/caas/manage-containers.md): View and update container configuration including status, image, ports, CPU/memory, autoscaling, API key authentication, environment variables, and logs in G...

## Container registry
- [Container registry](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/container-registry.md): Store and manage OCI-compatible Docker images and Helm charts with configurable per-user push/pull permissions across Gcore products.
- [Create a Container Registry](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/container-registry/create-container-registry.md): Create OCI-compatible Container Registry with region selection, storage limits, and user authentication for pushing/pulling images.
- [Manage container registries](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/container-registry/manage-container-registries.md): Adjust storage limits, manage images and artifacts, configure users, and delete container registries in Gcore Customer Portal.

## Managed logging
- [About managed logging](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/about-logging-as-a-service.md): Collect and store logs from Virtual Machines and Bare Metal servers via Kafka-compatible shippers, accessed through OpenSearch Dashboards for error detection...
- [Configure logging and view your logs](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/configure-logging-and-view-your-logs.md): Enable Managed Logging in region, create namespace and Kafka topic, generate credentials, configure log shipper (Fluent Bit, Filebeat, Logstash) with Kafka e...

## Install a log shipper
- [Install and configure Filebeat](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-filebeat.md): Install Filebeat and configure inputs, output.kafka, Kafka endpoint, SASL authentication, and SSL encryption for log shipping to Gcore Managed Logging.
- [Install and configure Fluent Bit](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-fluent-bit.md): Configure Fluent Bit [INPUT] and [OUTPUT] sections with log source, Kafka brokers, SASL authentication, and Gcore Logging endpoint to ship logs to OpenSearch...
- [Install and configure Fluentd](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-fluentd.md): Configure Fluentd with Kafka output plugin to stream logs to Gcore Managed Logging using brokers, SASL authentication, and topic settings.
- [Install and configure Logstash](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-logstash.md): Install Logstash and configure Kafka output plugin with SASL authentication to send logs to Gcore Managed Logging.
- [Prometheus exporter](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/prometheus-exporter.md): Export OpenSearch index metrics via Prometheus exporter endpoint using HTTPS Basic Authentication with Managed Logging credentials and gauge metrics.
- [View GCore managed logging logs in Grafana with OpenSearch plugin](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/view-opensearch-logs-in-Grafana.md): Integrate Gcore Managed Logging with Grafana using OpenSearch plugin for consolidated log visualization and alerting.
- [Manage cloud via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/manage-cloud-via-terraform.md): Configure Gcore Cloud resources using Terraform provider with API tokens, main.tf configuration files, and resource definitions for Virtual Machines, Kuberne...
- [Manage cloud resources via Ansible](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/manage-cloud-via-ansible.md): Install and configure Ansible with gcore/cloud collection to provision, deploy, and orchestrate Gcore Cloud resources via playbooks and modules.

## MCP Server
- [manage-ai-ml-via-mcp-server](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/mcp-server/manage-ai-ml-via-mcp-server.md)

- [manage-storage-via-mcp-server](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/mcp-server/manage-storage-via-mcp-server.md)

## Use cases
- [Set up a mailing server in GCore cloud VM](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/use-cases/set-up-a-mailing-server-in-gcore-cloud-vm.md): Configure Postfix and Dovecot mail server on GCore Cloud VM with port 25 SMTP unblocking, DNS records, and mailbox creation on Ubuntu/Debian.
